I want to show you how I use Mixpanel. I think it's the best tracking tool. However, every time I bring it up, somebody has another favorite. And that's just because there's a million bajillion tools in this space right now. What is the best tracking tool? The best tracking tool is the one you're actually going to use. You know, the one that's going to get you the critical metrics for your business. For me, that's Mixpanel. It's the easiest for me to use. It's the easiest for me to get my critical metrics. I know Google Analytics is free and can do everything the Mixpanel can, but somebody showed me how to frickin' do it. You know, I've tried. It's not as easy as it sounds. Just go set up goals and e-commerce and reports and all that good stuff and it costs thousands of dollars to get somebody to set it up. Where if you've watched on YouTube, I've got this how to set up Mixpanel, Brain Dead Easy. And now they have Mixpanel AutoTrack, which makes it even easier. You just click a button, tell it what you want at the track, and then bang, bang. It's tracking your whole funnel for you. And there's a lot more that Mixpanel can do. It's really amazing if you have software because it can tell you a lot of things about what your users are doing with your software. Or like if you have an app, like an iOS or Android app, it's amazing for that. It can, you know, so let's say you have tracking on your site, right? And then you have somebody that goes through one of your forms and opts in or maybe becomes a customer. Well, it knows who that person is backdated because it gives every user an ID. So as soon as that person opts into your form, you can then track it back to the first blog post they came in or when they came into your home page or whatever, which is really cool because when they then become a customer, you can then look back at the pattern and see, oh, okay, so this person, they became a lead here. And then before they came a lead, they read this blog post and that blog post. So you can like go back and see the whole history. You can do all the regular things like see what your, you know, how many page views you've had and you can sort it by a lot of other stuff. I'm not going to show you that. I think what you're mostly interested in is how I use it to track my funnel. So the first thing is, is you would use your normal link and then you're going to add UTM parameters to that link. So if I open up another tab and I go on scalable G links and I type in here, you know, and I can say this is from email and it was from a broadcast, not one of my honor responders. And it was from, you know, you can just put the date in here. So it's like, let's see, except 12 2016. All right, now you know exactly which email, which broadcast this came from. So I generate the link bang. All right, so now anybody who clicks that link in my email, I can then come back into MixPayable and I can make a funnel off of that link and see how many people bought from that specific email and you can set up a whole bunch of funnels in here. You can do a B testing, you can do live view live view is pretty cool. You see where people are coming in. Not all of these are set up accurately right now. I'm teaching my I've got a new guy in charge of data, you know, I'm trying to teach him to be like our data analyst. And so I'm teaching him about tracking and data analysis. So not everything is completely set up correctly. This one is fairly right. I know that this one is good. And so the way you set it up here is you go to edit funnel. And so you just put in the steps. So page view current URL is our home page. So everybody came in through our home page. And then they went to the sales page for this product. And then they went to the thank you page for this product. And for all you slicksters out there who think you're going to go and type in bulletproof native ads purchase, you still have to have a log in. So go ahead and type it in if you want to, but it's not going to do anything for you. If anything, it's going to show you our upsells. So that's how you do it. But the one problem I have with mixed panel is when you set that up, they have to go through that fall. It's not like it's counting. Oh, okay, so the person landed here and then went to here and so add that in there. No, it's it they have to go through all the steps. And so if we put only two steps like home page, and you know, the actual sale, it changes the numbers versus where if we just put the sales page, and the actual sale, it changes the numbers again, and then we put all three of them changes the numbers again. So you kind of have to, you know, decide on what's important to you. And for us, we're trying to figure out what are the ideal paths that our users are taking. Obviously, conversion is important to us. Obviously, ROI is important to us revenue is still our number one driving number. You know, we're no dummies, we love money. Like to roll around in my bed. Just kidding, not really. But you see here, you know, even with this scenario, we've got a 2.8% conversion rate from the sales page to the purchase. So for us, you know, you know, since revenues taking care of we're doing great with revenue. What's the next most important thing for us is to try and figure out what these ideal pathways are. And then through our blogs, the banners on our blogs, the pop ups to welcome mass to smart bars, the links and everything that we always marketers do to automate our marketing and our emails, and our activation sequences and consumption sequences. Once we know the pathway, the micro conversions that lead up to the conversion, then we can use all of those that are still of tools to guide them through that pathway and get them through the micro conversions. For example, Evernote has an email sequence, and they don't actually sell their product until day 17. And the reason why is because there is a bunch of micro conversions that they know if they get that person through these micro conversions, they're much more likely to buy. So they take them through a process to get them through the micro conversions, so that they'll finally get through the macro conversions. And it's about finding that ideal path that makes you the most amount of money. That's why we don't have a revenue problem is because we're focused on designing the ideal pathway and pushing people along the ideal pathway. Anyways, enough about that, I feel like a broken record. So this is how I set up Mixpanel. I don't really know how to do a lot of the other stuff. I've had people on my team set it up so that we can set up notifications. They show me about retention, I don't really get it. I definitely don't get formulas. There's a lot of value in here. They have webinars, they have amazing customer support. So if you really want to learn all the stuff, you can and there's so much that it can do, it can do surveys. The notifications thing is cool because you can design a notification to show up. Like if you know that somebody's visited page X and page Y and then lands on page Z, you can then show them a little pop up notification, giving them a coupon or whatever you want to do, you can trigger an email. There's so many things that notifications and surveys can do. And then obviously, A, B testing, you know what that does. But for me, it's really all about funnels. That's really why I use this. It's I feel it's one of the best products out there for easily being able to track your funnel. There's a lot of other ones where you have to place pixels and create new tracking links every every time. And like I said, if you know of an easier tool, a better tool, great, use that. Mix panel is the one that I feel does the best job for me. We're always looking at new tools as well and constantly trying to find what's the best thing for us right now is MixPanel because a priority for us is finding these ideal pathways.
